volumeTitle: The Little Carthaginian. Pseudolus. The Rope
label: The Little Carthaginian (Section 5.2.998b - Section 5.2.1065)

sections.label: Section 5.2.998b
sections.translation: He salutes us .

sections.label: Section 5.2.998c
sections.translation: Donni [*] .

sections.label: Section 5.2.998d
sections.translation: He intends to present you with some donation out of this ; what , I don ’ t know . Don ’ t you hear him promise ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.1000
sections.original: Saluta hunc rursus Punice verbis meis .
sections.translation: Salute him again in Punic , in my name .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1001
sections.original: Avo donni inquit hic tibi verbis suis .
sections.translation: Avo donni he tells me to say to you in his name .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1002
sections.translation: Mehar bocca [*] !

sections.label: Section 5.2.1002b
sections.original: Istuc tibi sit potius quam mihi .
sections.translation: Be that for yourself rather than me !

sections.label: Section 5.2.1003
sections.translation: What does he say ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.1003b
sections.translation: He declares that his box for his teeth is painful . Perhaps he takes us to be doctors .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1005
sections.original: Si ita est , nega esse ; nolo ego errare hospitem .
sections.translation: If it is so , tell him that we are not ; I don ’ t wish a stranger to be mistaken .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1006
sections.translation: Hear you .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1006b
sections.translation: Rufen nuco istam [*] .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1006c
sections.translation: This is my wish , that in fact everything should be explained to him just as it is . Ask him whether he has need of anything .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1008b
sections.translation: You who have got no girdle [*] , why have you come to this city , or what is it you seek ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.1010
sections.translation: Muphursa [*] .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1010b
sections.translation: What is it he says ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.1010c
sections.translation: Moin lechianna [*] .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1010d
sections.translation: Why has he come ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.1011
sections.translation: Don ’ t you hear ? He declares that he is wishful to give African mice to the Aediles as a show at the games .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1013
sections.translation: Lalech lachananim liminichot [*] .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1013b
sections.translation: What does he say now ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.1014
sections.original: Ligulas , canalis ait se advexisse et nuces :
sections.translation: He says he has brought latchets , water-channels [*] , and nuts ;

sections.label: Section 5.2.1015
sections.original: nunc orat , operam ut des sibi , ut ea veneant .
sections.translation: he ’ s now begging that you ’ ll lend him your assistance in having them sold .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1016
sections.translation: He is a merchant , I suppose ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.1016b
sections.translation: Is amar binam [*] .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1016c
sections.translation: What is it he says ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.1017
sections.translation: Palum erga dectha [*] .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1017b
sections.translation: Milphio , what is he saying now ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.1018
sections.original: Palas vendundas sibi ait et mergas datas ,
sections.translation: He says that he has got spades and forks [*] given him for sale ,

sections.label: Section 5.2.1020
sections.original: ut hortum fodiat atque ut frumentum metat .
sections.translation: for digging the garden and reaping the corn .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1021b
sections.translation: He wishes you to be informed of it , so that you mayn ’ t suppose that he has taken anything secretly and by stealth . He has really , I do believe , been sent here to your harvesting .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1023
sections.translation: Muphonium sucoraim [*] .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1023b
sections.translation: So there ! do take care , please , how you do what he ’ s begging of you .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1024b
sections.original: Quid ait aut quid orat ? expedi .
sections.translation: What is he saying , or what is he begging ? Explain it .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1025
sections.original: Sub cratim ut iubeas se supponi átque eo
sections.translation: For you to order him to be placed beneath a hurdle [*] , and for many stones to be heaped upon it , so as to put him to death .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1027
sections.translation: Gunebel balsamen ierasan !

sections.label: Section 5.2.1027b
sections.translation: Tell me what it is that he ’ s saying .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1028b
sections.original: Non hercle nunc quidem quicquam scio .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , now I really don ’ t at all know .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1029
sections.original: At ut scias , nunc dehinc latine iam loquar .
sections.translation: But that you may know , now from this moment henceforth will I speak Latin .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1030
sections.original: servom hercle te esse oportet et nequam et malum ,
sections.translation: ( To MILPHIO .) Upon my faith , you must be a worthless and bad servant , to be laughing at a person , a foreigner and a stranger .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1032
sections.original: At hercle te hominem et sycophantam et subdolum ,
sections.translation: But , i ’ faith , at yourself a person that ’ s both a swindler and a cheat , who have come here to take us in , you half-and-half Lybian , you double-tongue , just like a crawling reptile .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1035
sections.original: Maledicta hinc aufer , linguam compescas face .
sections.translation: Away hence with your abusiveness ! do restrain your tongue . You ’ ll keep it from uttering abuse , if you are prudent ; I don ’ t want you to be speaking harshly to my kinsmen . I was born at Carthage ; do you remember that .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1039
sections.translation: O my fellow-countryman , greetings to you !

sections.label: Section 5.2.1039b
sections.translation: And you , troth , whoever you are ;

sections.label: Section 5.2.1040
sections.original: et si quid opus est , quaeso , dic atque impera
sections.translation: and if you have need of anything , pray mention it , and command me for the sake of our common country .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1041b
sections.translation: I return you thanks ; but I ’ ve got a place of entertainment here ; I ’ m in search of the son of Antidamas ; do point me out Agorastocles , if you know him . Do you know any young man here named Agorastocles ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.1045
sections.translation: If , indeed , you are in search of the adopted son of Antidamas , I am the very person whom you are in search of .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1046b
sections.translation: Hah ! what ’ s that I hear ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.1047
sections.translation: That I am the son of Antidamas .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1047b
sections.translation: If so it is , if you would like to compare the token of hospitality [*] , see here , I ’ ve brought it .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1049
sections.original: Agedum huc ostende . est par probe , quam habeo domi .
sections.translation: Come then , show it here . ( He takes it in his hand , and looks at it .) It is exactly true ; for I ’ ve got the counterpart at home .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1050
sections.original: O mi hospes , salve multum . nam mihi tuos pater
sections.translation: O my host , hail to you right earnestly ; for it was your father , then , Antidamas , that was my own and my father ’ s guest ; this was my token of hospitality with him .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1053
sections.original: Ergo hic apud me hospitium tibi praebebitur .
sections.translation: Then here at my house shall hospitality be shown you ; for I don ’ t reject either Hospitality or Carthage ,

sections.label: Section 5.2.1055
sections.translation: from which I sprang .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1055b
sections.original: Di dent tibi omnes quae velis .
sections.translation: May the Gods grant you all you may desire . How say you ? How could it happen that you were born at Carthage , but had a father of Aetolia here ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.1058
sections.original: Surruptus sum illinc . hic me Antidama hospes tuos
sections.translation: I was stolen away from there ; this Antidamas , your guest , bought me , and adopted me as his son .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1060
sections.translation: He himself , likewise , was adopted by Demarchus , But about him I say no more , and return to you . Tell me , do you at all remember the names of your parents ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.1063b
sections.translation: I remember my father and my mother ’ s name .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1063c
sections.translation: Repeat them , then , to me , to see if I know them , perchance , or if they are relatives of mine .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1065
sections.original: Ampsigura mater mihi fuit , Iahon pater .
sections.translation: Ampsigura was my mother , and Iachon my father .
